Thesis deteriorating: While metrics remain strong (29% ROIC, 10.4% FCF, +47% RG), DLO is a FastGrower in a severely overcrowded Lynch category (8 current vs. 4 max cap). Portfolio must shed FastGrowers to restore balance. DLO's weaker moat vs. GDDY (duplicative LatAm fintech play) makes it the logical FastGrower to trim. Sell to create slot for underweighted sectors (Financial Services, Healthcare, Energy, Consumer Cyclical). Held 15d; early exit justified by structural category overweight.

Thesis deteriorating: Despite exceptional 61% ROIC, ESTC is a FastGrower contributing to overcrowded Lynch category (8 current vs. 4 max). PEG 0.50 and +17% RG are excellent, but portfolio must shed FastGrowers. ESTC's 5.0% FCF yield is weakest in portfolio and insufficient to justify premium ROIC valuation at -4% MoS. Sell to rebalance Lynch categories and create dry powder. Held 14d; category constraint overrides individual quality.

Thesis deteriorating: NICE is a Turnaround (7 current vs. 3 max cap) acquired TODAY at $105.60. While IV Discount profile and 10.2% FCF yield are solid, ROIC 13% is marginal (only 8% above 12% threshold) and +9% RG is below 10%+ portfolio standard. Most critically, portfolio is violating Lynch Turnaround cap by 4 positions. NICE must be shed immediately to correct structural overweight. Held 0d; category overflow constraint is binding.

Thesis deteriorating: GDDY is a FastGrower (8 current vs. 4 max cap) acquired TODAY. GDDY competes with DLO in fintech/LatAm space but has weaker growth (+8% vs. +47%). Sell to immediately relieve Lynch category overweight. Thesis deteriorating: Sold DLO/ESTC/GDDY on 2026-04-23 due to Lynch overconcentration (FastGrowers were 8, cap is 4). PTCT was added as 'replacement' but portfolio still violates constraints: FastGrowers now 5 (should be ≤4), Turnarounds 7 (should be ≤3), Stalwarts 6 (should be ≤5). PTCT is FastGrower—keeping it perpetuates the violation. While metrics (37% ROIC, 115% RG) are strong, the REASON to own it (as a replacement) never solved the structural problem. Sell to free slot and reduce FastGrower count to 4. Only 2 days held, but the overweight is the thesis failure.

Thesis broken by portfolio structure, not fundamentals: Financial Services sector is at hard cap (4 positions: HIG, TRV, CB, ALL). Portfolio rule explicitly forbids a 5th sector position. ALL is the weakest of the four on ROIC (21% vs. HIG 29%, TRV 47%, CB 35%) and has lowest FCF yield (17.9% vs. TRV 17.8%, but lowest quality overall). Only 2 days held, but adding it violated sector cap rule. Sell to return to 4-position Financial Services max and free capital for diversification into under-represented sectors (Industrials, Consumer Cyclical, Healthcare).
